## [00:00 - 03:00]

This film is about how to coach a couple to develop presence, playfulness and trust. Many couples once enjoyed great love and passion, but have noticed a decline in their playfulness, passion and trust, and they blame it on their age or the age of the relationship. The truth is that passion does not depend on age. It depends on the experience of total emotional presence between two people. We begin with Tony Robbins teaching a room of 2,000 people when he gets a question from Richard and Elaine, a couple who had felt they had lost their spark. Tony guides this couple and the audience as a whole through an exercise for reclaiming your passion and your bond. So what's your name? Where are you from? Richard from Shadow Washington. Wow, nice to meet you. Come on along with me. So Richard, tell us, what was this relationship or this experience you've had that was so magnificent, what was it like? It was my wedding day and the emotions were very overwhelming. My family and friends were all there. It was a beautiful warm spring, sunny day. It was in an English style garden. It was just, it was beautiful. It was just everything that my wife and I both wanted to have for that day. It's beautiful. It was first wedding for both of us, even though we were both older. Wonderful, wonderful. And that feeling you've had, that experience you had of that moment, what would you be willing to do to have that be around all the time? Well, therein lies the problem. During this process, something in me just shut down. I've never had this happen before. And all of a sudden it's like right now I'm numb. Through this film, I will guide you step by step on how to coach a couple so they can recover the joy and passion that they once had. The first step is to ask the husband why that joy and passion was lost. And what did you say was preventing you from having it be that way all the time? And so I'm very comfortable going into the feminine energy. Yes. And I've been spending way too much time there. Richard talks about how he has not stepped up as a man and how he is too comfortable in his feminine energy.
